description: You are a security engineer who must perform a routine credential-rotation for a small in-house application whose code lives in /home/user/app. CURRENT STATE (before you start): • The directory /home/user/app/config already exists. • Inside it there is ONE plaintext credentials file named /home/user/app/config/credentials.env that still contains legacy secrets. • There is **no** other file in that directory (no backups, no log). WHAT YOU MUST DO (5–10 shell commands total): 1. Back up the existing credentials.env file into /home/user/app/config/credentials_legacy.env – the backup must be an exact byte-for-byte copy of the original. 2. Replace (overwrite) /home/user/app/config/credentials.env with a new file that contains exactly the three lines shown below (no extra spaces, blank lines or trailing newline after the last line): DB_USER=appuser DB_PASSWORD=N3wS3cureP@ssw0rd API_TOKEN=abcd1234efgh5678 3. Create (or append to, if it already existed) a log file /home/user/app/config/rotation.log containing ONE line that records this rotation in the following fixed, testable format (ISO-8601 timestamp followed by a literal dash and message). Use **exactly** the timestamp given below so the automated tests can verify it deterministically: 2023-09-01T12:00:00Z - ROTATED credentials.env -> credentials_legacy.env VERIFICATION RULES (used by the automated grader): • /home/user/app/config/credentials_legacy.env must exist and must be byte-identical to the original credentials.env that you found. • /home/user/app/config/credentials.env must exist and contain exactly the three new key-value lines in the specified order, with no additional whitespace or newline at EOF. • /home/user/app/config/rotation.log must exist and contain exactly the single line shown above (including the final “.env” suffix), followed by a single trailing newline. • The original credentials must never appear in the new credentials.env. • No other files should be created or removed inside /home/user/app/config. IMPORTANT: • You have normal user permissions only; do not use sudo. • Accomplish the task using 5-10 well-chosen shell commands. • Do not print the new credentials to stdout; only manipulate the files.
truth:
<!-- Initial file that already exists before the agent starts -->
    File: /home/user/app/config/credentials.env
    Content (exact, 3 lines, no final newline):
        DB_USER=legacyuser
        DB_PASSWORD=L0ngOldPass
        API_TOKEN=oldtoken999

    <!-- Files that must exist after successful task completion -->
    Expected final files and their exact contents:

    1. /home/user/app/config/credentials_legacy.env
       Content (must match the *initial* credentials.env byte-for-byte):
        DB_USER=legacyuser
        DB_PASSWORD=L0ngOldPass
        API_TOKEN=oldtoken999

    2. /home/user/app/config/credentials.env
       Content (exact, 3 lines, no extra newline after the last line):
        DB_USER=appuser
        DB_PASSWORD=N3wS3cureP@ssw0rd
        API_TOKEN=abcd1234efgh5678

    3. /home/user/app/config/rotation.log
       Content (exactly one line, ending with a single newline):
        2023-09-01T12:00:00Z - ROTATED credentials.env -> credentials_legacy.env\n
